eventMetadata.context_description: Connor McDavid leads trader consensus for the Hart Trophy at 53% implied probability after topping the 2025-26 regular season scoring charts with a league-high 138 points (48 goals, 90 assists), capping a late-season tear that secured the Art Ross Trophy and propelled the Oilers into playoff contention amid roster challenges. Nikita Kucherov follows at 29% with 130 points (44G, 86A), earning NHL.com panel favoritism for carrying the Lightning to the postseason despite defensive injuries. Nathan MacKinnon holds third at 17.5% on 127 points (53G, 74A) and elite two-way play for Colorado, though his Avalanche secured a top seed without matching McDavid's raw dominance. Macklin Celebrini's rookie surge nets 2.8%, but voters prioritize established impact; finalists loom May 8.

markets.question: Will CA Platense win on 2026-05-07?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 7, 2026
If CA Platense wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CA Platense vs. CA Peñarol
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Copa Libertadores game, scheduled for Thursday, May 7, 2026 between CA Platense and CA Peñarol.

eventMetadata.context_description: CA Platense enters as trader consensus slight favorite at 46% implied probability for their Copa Libertadores Group E home clash against Peñarol, buoyed by a 2-1 upset victory in the first leg at Montevideo that vaulted them to second in the standings behind Corinthians. Peñarol's recent domestic losses and mounting injury crisis—key doubts over Ferreira, Darias, and Ángulo, plus long-term absences like Leonardo Fernández (knee) and Nahuel Herrera (shoulder)—have eroded their away threat to 24%, while conserving players for this pivotal fixture underscores its stakes for top-two qualification. Platense plans a near-identical lineup to their winning XI, with only minor tweaks like Gómez for Amarfil, amplifying home form in a closely contested matchup where draw pricing at 30.5% reflects defensive resilience on both sides.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the #3 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Girona qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Champions League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Champions League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 LaLiga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Champions League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or LaLiga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 360-379 tweets from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 5 12:00 PM ET to May 12, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters implied probabilities tightly around 180-259 X posts by Elon Musk from May 5-12, reflecting his recent moderated posting pace of 20-28 daily in late April—down sharply from March's 42/day average—amid distractions like xAI's Grok 4.3 rollout and post-Tesla Q1 earnings focus on April 22. April 30 logged 23 posts on Starlink expansions and politics, but May 1 dipped to just 7, underscoring volatility that keeps top outcomes neck-and-neck. Key swing factors include potential spikes from viral political commentary, SpaceX updates, or platform announcements, with no confirmed events yet but high uncertainty in Musk's engagement-driven social media habits.

markets.question: Will Mike Kennealy win the 2026 Massachusetts Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Massachusetts Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's commanding 70% victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention last weekend secured the MassGOP endorsement and propelled his odds to 84%, reflecting trader consensus on his strong delegate support and self-funded campaign as a former Abiomed CEO. Brian Shortsleeve narrowly qualified for the September 1 primary ballot with 15.5% of delegate votes, positioning him as the primary challenger at 9.5%, bolstered by his Marine veteran background and prior MBTA leadership. Mike Kennealy's 14% share fell short of the 15% threshold, prompting his campaign suspension and relegating him to 1.9%, underscoring the convention's decisive role in narrowing the Republican gubernatorial primary field amid limited polling data.

markets.question: Will Chelsea Clinton be the democratic nominee for NY-12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the open Democratic primary for New York's 12th Congressional District on June 23—sparked by Rep. Jerry Nadler's retirement—trader consensus favors Assemblymember Micah Lasher at 44.5% implied probability, driven by former NYC Mayor Michael Bloomberg's super PAC injecting over $4.3 million in pro-Lasher ads and mailers touting his policy experience with Gov. Kathy Hochul and Nadler, plus Nadler's February endorsement. Assemblymember Alex Bores holds 32%, resilient despite $2.4 million in attacks from an OpenAI-backed PAC (offset by $1.2 million pro-Bores from Anthropic), while Jack Schlossberg's 16% reflects Kennedy name recognition amid mixed early polls showing fluid leads. Recent April candidate forums at 92nd Street Y and ACS heightened visibility, but no independent polls since mid-April underscore uncertainty in this crowded, high-spending race.

markets.question: Will Yoo Dong-soo win the 2026 Incheon mayoral election?
markets.description:
The Incheon mayoral election is scheduled to take place in Incheon, South Korea on June 3, 2026 to elect the next mayor of Incheon. 

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic Party of Korea candidate Park Chan-dae commands overwhelming trader consensus at 95% implied probability for the June 3 Incheon mayoral election, driven by the ruling party's landslide in the recent general election where it captured most Incheon National Assembly seats, signaling strong local momentum under President Lee Jae-myung. Consistent polling averages since early 2026 show Park leading incumbent People Power Party Mayor Yoo Jeong-bok by 20-25 points, bolstered by Park's official candidacy announcement on April 22 and party leader visits amplifying campaign energy. While Yoo declared his third-term bid on April 29 emphasizing administrative experience, no major shifts have narrowed the gap. Scenarios like a late scandal, voter turnout favoring incumbents, or national opposition surge could challenge this, though structural advantages favor Park absent disruptions.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Los Angeles be between 70-71°F on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Los Angeles International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Los Angeles International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/los-angeles/KLAX.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits probabilities across 64-69°F outcomes at 35.5% each, reflecting National Weather Service forecasts and model ensembles like GFS and ECMWF projecting a high near 66-68°F for downtown Los Angeles on May 4 amid persistent coastal marine layer. This low marine inversion layer, fueled by cool Pacific sea surface temperatures and moderate onshore flow (5-10 mph), traps stratus clouds through midday, capping solar heating and keeping highs below seasonal averages of 72-74°F seen in historical May 4 data. Differentiating factors include burn-off timing—delayed clearing under stronger winds favors 64-65°F, while earlier dissipation in lighter flow pushes toward 68-69°F—highlighting short-range uncertainty ahead of evening forecast updates.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,000 (HIGH) in June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for June 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of June?
events.description: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of June 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: The S&P 500 has notched record highs near 7,230, propelled by resilient corporate earnings—particularly from Big Tech—and the Federal Reserve's April 29 decision to hold the federal funds rate steady at 3.5%-3.75% amid cooling inflation pressures and stable unemployment at 4.3% in March. This monetary policy pause, despite notable internal dissent, bolsters market-implied rate path expectations, fostering risk-on sentiment with 10% monthly gains. Traders eye May CPI data on June 10 and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting as pivotal catalysts, alongside Q2 earnings previews and Treasury yield trends, to gauge potential upside or volatility through end-June resolution thresholds.

markets.question: BNB Up or Down - May 2, 8:45AM-8:50AM ET
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the BNB price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BNB/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/bnb-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BNB/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will the April 2026 unemployment rate be 4.4%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in the Employment Situation Report for April 2026.

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for the month in question.

The relevant data release is scheduled for May 8, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. This market will resolve as soon as the relevant data is issued. Any revisions to the data after the first release will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Realistic challenges include a rare late-afternoon convective heat burst pushing beyond 33°C before sunset, though cooling sea breezes and diurnal cycles make this improbable; final CWA daily report expected post-midnight.

markets.question: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. RC Celta de Vigo: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game between Club Atlético de Madrid and RC Celta de Vigo, scheduled for May 9 at 12:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Club Atlético de Madrid and RC Celta de Vigo combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Home advantage and table stakes shape trader consensus.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 260-279 tweets from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 28 12:00 PM ET to May 5, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

Mikie Sherrill, saw robust progressive turnout favoring Mejia after her competitive primary win, with no reported recounts or ballot disputes emerging in the ensuing two weeks. The commanding position stems from the district's entrenched Democratic lean and low special election turnout amplifying base mobilization. Realistic challenges would require substantial late provisional ballots or audit discrepancies to shrink the margin below 20%, scenarios deemed improbable given the wide lead and procedural stability ahead of final state certification.

markets.question: Jimmy Lai released by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jimmy Lai is released from custody by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Lai is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Lai is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Lai to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Extradition of Lai to another country where he remains in custody will also not count.

Temporary outings from prison for purposes such as testifying in court, while still under the custody of correctional authorities, will not count as a release.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from relevant government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

His legal team confirmed in early March that he will not appeal, closing domestic judicial avenues and solidifying his detention in Stanley Prison, where releases under the NSL remain exceedingly rare absent political intervention. Trader consensus at 97.3% for "No" reflects this finality, though humanitarian parole or diplomatic pressure—such as UK or US negotiations—could theoretically prompt early release by June 30 amid international calls for clemency.

markets.question: Will Silver (SI) settle over $75 on the final trading day of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Silver futures on the final trading day of June 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

markets.question: Will Meta have the best AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company which owns the model which has the highest arena rank based off the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with the style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will "Running Point: Season 2" be the top global Netflix show this week?
markets.description:
Netflix is expected to update its global Top 10 TV shows list on top10.netflix.com on Tuesday, May 5, 2026, 3:00 PM ET, reflecting viewership from the previous week (Monday to Sunday).

This market will resolve based on which show this update ranks as the #1 global Netflix show.

The ranking is based on total views globally, as reported by Netflix for TV shows (English only).

If the top10.netflix.com update does not occur by May 8, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Man on Fire: Season 1's explosive Netflix debut on April 30 has propelled it to an 84.5% implied probability as the top global TV show this week, dominating daily worldwide charts like FlixPatrol's May 1 rankings with 740 points ahead of rivals. Yahya Abdul-Mateen II's commanding lead in this high-octane action-crime thriller, a reimagining of the classic tale, has fueled massive viewer engagement through strong promotional buzz and genre appeal, eclipsing last week's frontrunner Unchosen (now at 5.5%). Should I Marry A Murderer?, a gripping true-crime docuseries topping U.S. lists at 7.9%, trails due to narrower international traction, while holdovers like Running Point: Season 2 linger low amid shifting streaming metrics. Traders eye Netflix's weekly Top 10 update for final confirmation, as debut momentum often secures the crown.

markets.question: Will Bitcoin reach $84,000 April 27-May 3?
markets.description:

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" candles selected on the top bar.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve's April 29, 2026, FOMC meeting held the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%–3.75% for the third consecutive decision, despite an 8-4 dissent—the highest in years—signaling internal hawkish pressure amid a March CPI surge to 3.3% year-over-year driven by geopolitical oil shocks. Trader consensus on Polymarket, pricing an 82.5% implied probability of no rate hike through 2026, reflects the Committee's assessment of solid economic expansion, stable 4.3% unemployment, and balanced risks, prioritizing data-dependent monitoring over immediate tightening. Elevated Treasury yields and resilient labor data temper hike expectations, though prolonged inflation or Strait disruptions could shift odds; watch May nonfarm payrolls and June FOMC for catalysts.

markets.question: Will Jalen Duren win the 2026 Eastern Conference Finals MVP?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 Eastern Conference Finals Most Valuable Player award for the 2026 NBA Playoffs.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by NBA rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 NBA Playoffs are cancelled, postponed after June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) hit (HIGH) $90 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 77.5% chance against the U.S. enacting an AI safety bill before 2027, driven by persistent congressional gridlock despite recent narrow advancements. On April 30, the Senate Commerce Committee advanced the bipartisan GUARD Act and CHATBOT Act targeting child safety from AI chatbots and deepfakes, but these fall short of comprehensive safety prohibitions or whistleblower mandates envisioned in broader proposals like the AI Guardrails Act, stuck in early stages since March. The Trump administration's March National Policy Framework called for federal preemption over state AI laws and innovation-focused rules, yet no floor votes have materialized amid partisan divides and midterm distractions. States' 600+ AI bills highlight federal inaction, eroding momentum for national legislation.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a SpaceX IPO closing market cap exceeding $2.0 trillion at 57.5% implied probability, driven by the company's April 2026 confidential S-1 filing and leaked prospectus revealing Starlink's $4.42 billion operating income in 2025 alongside surging $20 billion-plus capital expenditures for Starship reusability and xAI-integrated orbital data centers. markets.question: Game Handicap: JDG (-1.5) vs Team WE (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Team WE and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 2 at 3:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "JD Gaming" if JD Gaming wins 2 or more games than Team WE in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Team WE". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Game Handicap: JDG (-1.5) vs Team WE (+1.5)

events.title: LoL: Team WE vs JD Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Team WE and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 2 at 3:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team WE" if Team WE win the match against JD Gaming.

This market will resolve to "JD Gaming" if JD Gaming win the match against Team WE.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: JD Gaming holds a commanding edge over winless Team WE in their LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 rematch, building on a clean 2-0 sweep just last week on April 25 amid double round-robin play. WE languishes at 0-8 series (0-16 games) in the top group, swept by every opponent including recent losses to BLG and AL, reflecting poor macro execution and teamfights. JDG sits 4th at 5-3 with a four-series win streak over NIP and iG, bolstered by Knight's midlane dominance and Bin's top pressure under patch 26.7's blue-side advantage. Playoff implications loom larger for JDG, while WE faces elimination risk despite Hard Fearless Draft potential for upsets.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Warsaw be 19°C or below on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Warsaw Chopin Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 3 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Warsaw Chopin Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pl/warsaw/EPWA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Brest victory at 46% implied probability ahead of their Ligue 1 home clash against Strasbourg, reflecting strong home form at Stade Francis-Le Blé and an unbeaten record in the last two head-to-heads—a 2-1 away win in November 2025 and a 0-0 draw earlier that year. Strasbourg, sitting 8th with 46 points from 30 matches, holds competitive 34% odds buoyed by an unbeaten run in five games including recent away wins, but faces mounting injury concerns with Aaron Anselmino (hamstring), Valentin Barco (ankle), Guéla Doué (virus), and others sidelined, thinning their squad depth. Brest (11th, 38 points) contends with Bradley Locko out (hamstring) and suspensions, yet the matchup projects as tightly contested with a 33% draw chance amid mid-table stakes.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the U.S. military operation capturing Nicolás Maduro on January 3, 2026, Delcy Rodríguez was sworn in as acting president by Venezuela's Supreme Court, with the Trump administration formally recognizing her as head of state in March and resuming direct commercial flights on May 1 amid economic thaw and sanctions relief. Trader consensus nevertheless prices Maduro at 64.5% to remain listed as Venezuela's leader by year-end in official government directories, reflecting unresolved constitutional transition, UN ambiguity on legitimacy, and no elections scheduled despite opposition leader María Corina Machado's stated intent to return by December and push for polls. Rodríguez trails at 23% on interim stability signals, while Machado holds 8.5% amid calls for democratic renewal.

markets.question: Putin and Zelenskyy shake hands by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelenskyy shake hands by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the handshake to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, there must be video or photographic evidence.

The resolution source for this market will be photographic or digital evidence verified by a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Putin and Zelenskyy shake hands by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelenskyy shake hands by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the handshake to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, there must be video or photographic evidence.

The resolution source for this market will be photographic or digital evidence verified by a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 96.2% for Putin and Zelenskyy shaking hands by June 30, reflecting entrenched positions amid ongoing hostilities and stalled diplomatic efforts. Recent Russian drone barrages, including over 400 strikes injuring civilians in western Ukraine within the last day, underscore persistent escalation rather than de-escalation signals. Zelenskyy is seeking U.S. clarification on Putin's short-term May 9 Victory Day ceasefire proposal floated last week, while proposing a long-term truce himself, but the Kremlin insists any leader meeting would only finalize pre-agreed terms—absent current political will from Kyiv. U.S.-mediated talks remain stalled by the Iran conflict and unmet preconditions, with no summit scheduled despite Zelenskyy's openness to neutral venues like Azerbaijan. A dramatic breakthrough, such as binding concessions or external mediation success before June 30, could shift odds, though historical patterns favor continuation of the deadlock.

markets.question: Will CS Cienciano vs. CD Comerciantes Unidos end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 2, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. markets.question: Theo FDV above $1B one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Theo's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Theo (https://x.com/Theo_Network) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Glenn Grothman, who secured a 22-point victory in 2024 with 61% of the vote in this R+8 district where Donald Trump won 58% in the presidential race, drives trader consensus toward an 82% implied probability of a Republican hold in the WI-06 House race. The district's Safe Republican rating from forecasters like Cook Political Report and Sabato's Crystal Ball underscores historical double-digit GOP margins since Grothman's 2015 tenure began. A crowded eight-candidate Democratic primary—featuring Amanda Bell, Kelly Brown, and others—lacks a clear frontrunner, while independents like fire captain Michael Thurow pose minimal threat. With the June 1 filing deadline passed and August 11 primaries looming, no polls or major shifts have emerged in recent weeks to challenge this positioning.

markets.description:

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in New York City be between 52-53°F on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in National Weather Service ensemble forecasts, with GFS and ECMWF models clustering overnight lows around 48-53°F at Central Park (KNYC) on May 4 amid post-frontal clearing after recent rain chances through May 2-3. The closely matched 21% implied probabilities for 48-49°F, 50-51°F, and 52-53°F stem from variability in predicted cloud cover and light winds overnight May 3-4, which modulate radiational cooling potential— fuller skies favor upper-50s, while radiative minima under clear conditions push toward mid-40s. Slightly below the early-May climatological low of 51°F due to lingering cool upper trough, new 00Z/12Z model runs on May 3 could sharpen differentiation ahead of resolution based on official observed minimum.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Oliver Adams Larkin at 46.5% over incumbent Jared Moskowitz at 26.5% in the FL-23 Democratic primary, reflecting progressive momentum in this closely contested race set for August 18. Larkin's recent national endorsement from the Democratic Socialists of America—the first for federal office in 2026—along with support from the Progressive Change Campaign Committee, has amplified his union-backed platform advocating Medicare for All, a $25 minimum wage, and Green New Deal policies. A March Center for Strategic Politics poll showed Larkin leading 49%-36% after voters heard balanced bios, highlighting dissatisfaction with Moskowitz's AIPAC ties and pro-Israel stance amid calls to condition U.S. aid. Moskowitz's incumbency offers fundraising edges, but limited town halls and constituent engagement have fueled challenger gains, with media appearances like Joy Reid boosting Larkin's visibility.

markets.question: Will Travis Kelce retire before next season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Travis Kelce retires from the NFL before the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An official announcement from Kelce that he is retiring will qualify even if his official retirement has not yet occurred, as long as the announced retirement is stated to take effect before the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season. Announcements that Kelce will retire after the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Travis Kelce, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty at 96% implied probability that Travis Kelce will not retire before the 2026 NFL season, anchored by his March signing of a three-year, $57.7 million Chiefs contract extension with $12 million guaranteed for next year, effectively locking in his return as the team's Pro Bowl tight end. Chiefs GM Brett Veach revealed on May 1 that Kelce committed just a day after the 2025 season finale—following a playoff miss—declaring he "wasn't going out like this," underscoring his enduring passion alongside Patrick Mahomes. Despite turning 37, no injury concerns or roster moves signal exit; rare catalysts like a major injury or personal shift could alter this, but the deal's structure provides strong continuity.

markets.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas wins 3 or more games than Toronto KOI in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Toronto KOI". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)

events.title: Call of Duty: Toronto KOI vs OpTic Texas (BO5) - Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers
events.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Toronto KOI" if Toronto KOI win the match against OpTic Texas.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas win the match against Toronto KOI.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpTic Texas holds a clear edge over Toronto KOI heading into their BO5 in the CDL Stage 3 Major Qualifiers, buoyed by a dominant 3-1 elimination win in the Minor I tournament on April 25, featuring masterclass Hardpoint outings from Mercules and Shotzzy on maps like Scar and Den. Both squads advanced undefeated at 2-0 in early Major 3 play—KOI with a sharp 6-1 map tally (86% win rate) showing rebound form post-Kips addition, while OpTic sits at 6-2 (75%) amid their prior league win streak. Recent scrims proved competitive (e.g., 4-3, 5-5 splits), but OpTic's veteran synergy (Huke, Shotzzy, Dashy, Mercules) excels in Hardpoint and Overload control, with KOI's newer lineup vulnerable to map vetoes and S&D execution. Trader consensus reflects OpTic's head-to-head momentum and qualifier consistency.

markets.question: Will Steve Hilton finish first in the 2026 California Governor primary election?
markets.description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the most valid votes in this primary election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors billionaire Tom Steyer at 36.5% to claim first place in California's June 2 nonpartisan top-two gubernatorial primary, driven by his unmatched self-funding and outspending of rivals amid a fragmented Democratic field. Former HHS Secretary Xavier Becerra has surged to 31.5% implied probability after Rep. Eric Swalwell's April scandal-forced withdrawal redistributed votes, leading the late-April Gudelunas Strategies poll at 24% versus Steve Hilton's 23%. Republican Hilton trails closely at 29.5%, buoyed by Emerson polling leads from GOP consolidation among undecideds exceeding 20%. Escalating attacks, including Becerra's May 1 coded criticisms of Steyer, highlight tensions, while upcoming debates could separate leaders in this volatile race.

markets.question: Will John Thune be the next Senate Majority Leader?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the individual who is announced as the next Senate Majority Leader after the November 3, 2026, U.S. General Election.

This market will remain open until January 3, 2027, at which point it will resolve based on the first announcement of the next Senate Majority Leader from whichever party holds the majority in the U.S. Senate.

If no Majority is established and a leader is not announced by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the next Senate Majority Leader reflects a razor-thin projected balance of power following the 2026 midterms, where Republicans defend 22 seats against Democrats' 13 amid battlegrounds like Maine, Michigan, North Carolina, Georgia, Ohio, and Texas. Chuck Schumer edges John Thune due to prediction markets implying a slight Democratic edge (around 51%) from historical midterm penalties on the president's party—exacerbated by sagging Trump approval ratings tied to high gas prices and foreign policy uncertainties—and Democratic generic ballot leads, though GOP incumbency advantages keep it competitive. The race stays tight amid internal dynamics, including Brian Schatz's viability as a Schumer challenger and low-odds GOP alternatives like John Barrasso. Yesterday's suspension of Maine Gov. Janet Mills' Senate bid—Schumer's prized recruit against Susan Collins—drew party criticism of his recruiting, potentially narrowing Democrats' path without yet moving odds; upcoming primaries, polls, and economic shifts could create separation by clarifying control.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 160-179 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket fragments across 1000-1200 tweet bins for Elon Musk's May posting volume, with 1160-1199 leading narrowly at 7.4% implied probability, closely trailed by 1040-1079 (7.0%) and 1000-1039 (6.5%), signaling high uncertainty in his variable cadence of 30-40 daily posts consistent with resolved April markets averaging 26-43 per day. May 1 saw an anomalous drop to 7 tweets amid xAI's Grok 4.3 launch—featuring aggressive pricing and voice advancements—but May 2 rebounded with 10+ posts on Starlink's Papua New Guinea rollout, demographic crises, and political firestorms. Competitive dynamics hinge on event-driven spikes from SpaceX milestones, Tesla buzz, or viral cultural debates versus lulls during AI deep dives, with crowds' real-money bets pricing normalization by month-end.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in London be 8°C on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors 11°C as London's lowest temperature on May 2 (98.9% implied probability), driven by early morning observations at key stations like Heathrow recording 11°C around 05:00 BST amid mist, per Met Office and BBC Weather data. Updated forecasts from the Met Office (as of 07:46) and BBC project daytime highs near 22°C with thundery showers and light southerly winds (5-7 mph) introducing cloud cover overnight, which traps heat and limits radiative cooling—preventing drops below 11°C despite a projected nighttime low around 10-13°C in models. Persistent rain from the south further insulates against colder air advection. Realistic challenges include unexpected clearing skies enabling sharper cooling to 10°C, though model ensembles show low agreement on such divergence; monitor hourly updates through midnight for final observations.

markets.question: Will Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) hit (HIGH) $435 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Microsoft's Q3 fiscal 2026 earnings on April 29 showcased robust Microsoft Cloud growth, with revenue hitting $82.9 billion—18% year-over-year—beating estimates amid surging Azure and AI demand, yet shares dipped post-report due to guidance for $190 billion in 2026 capital expenditures driven by soaring memory costs. Trading at approximately $414 per share as of May 1 close, MSFT reflects YTD pressure from tech sector volatility but trades at an attractive 26x forward earnings. Analyst consensus points to a $550–$590 average 12-month price target, signaling upside potential. Traders eye the May 21 dividend ex-date and July's Q4 earnings for catalysts, alongside broader Nasdaq trends and AI capex execution risks.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

This mid-April surge to an all-time high of 61% followed polls showing Democrats leading generic ballots by 2-5 points and early 2028 matchups, signaling anti-incumbent sentiment despite VP JD Vance's lead in Republican nominee odds at 39%. The open-seat race, with Trump term-limited, heightens uncertainty, with 2026 midterms poised as a key test of party momentum ahead of primaries and the November 2028 vote.

eventMetadata.context_description: Athletico Paranaense hold a strong position near the top of the Serie A table around fifth place after recent home wins including 2-0 over Cruzeiro and 2-1 in another league fixture, though they fell 3-2 at Fluminense last month, showcasing their solid form at Arena da Baixada where they thrive with home advantage. Historically, Grêmio lead head-to-head 19-10-5, but trader consensus weighs Athletico's momentum and Grêmio's defensive vulnerabilities ahead of the May 2 clash.

markets.question: Will an Iranian agent be charged in the US by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or announces a criminal indictment of any individual alleged to be acting as an agent of the Iranian government between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Iranian agent” refers to any individual alleged to be acting on behalf of, under the direction of, or in coordination with the Iranian government (including its military or intelligence services) to conduct espionage, collect intelligence, or perform other covert operations that benefit Iran.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 77.5% implied probability for an Iranian agent—such as an IRGC operative or spy engaged in espionage or assassination plots—being charged by the Department of Justice by May 31, reflecting the absence of qualifying DOJ or FBI indictments in recent weeks. The March conviction of Pakistani national Asif Merchant in an Iran-linked murder-for-hire scheme targeting Donald Trump marked the last major such case, but no new charges against Iranian intelligence personnel have followed. Recent actions, including the April 19 arrest of Iranian national Shamim Mafi at LAX for sanctions violations tied to brokering arms sales to Sudan and the April 25 indictment of Jafar Tafakori for migrant smuggling, involve economic crimes rather than explicit agency allegations. Amid heightened U.S. sanctions on Iran weapons networks and ongoing regional tensions, including a naval blockade, traders see insufficient momentum for a qualifying development before the deadline.

markets.question: Game Handicap: KT (-1.5) vs HANJIN BRION (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between KT Rolster and HANJIN BRION in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for May 3 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "KT Rolster" if KT Rolster wins 2 or more games than HANJIN BRION in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "HANJIN BRION". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
